meta.subject: Frank Lepik
meta.lineage: Frank Lepik → Fred Charles Lepick Sr. → Jennive Lepick Mattingly → Leroy Mattingly Jr. → Hunter Spence (maternal line)
meta.research_scope: Exact Bohemian/Moravian village of origin; parents; patrilineal line through Czech parish records

biography.birth_place_note: US census label 'Bohemia' was generic for all Czech lands including Moravia and Czech Silesia; does not exclude Frýdek-Místek / Moravian-Silesian origin
biography.buried: Kennekuk Cemetery, Horton, Brown County, Kansas
biography.immigration_note: Arrived alone; wife Mary Mikeska arrived 1871 with her parents
biography.religion_source: Married at St. Joseph Catholic Church, Everest KS, 13 Jan 1885

marriage.place: St. Joseph Catholic Church, Everest, Kansas
marriage.familysearch_citation: Brown County marriage Book D, image 59

parents.note: WikiTree Lepik-8 explicitly states [father unknown] and [mother unknown]. No US or Czech record in this research pass names Frank's parents.
parents.inference: Frank was ~19 at emigration (1881). Occupation as shoemaker throughout Kansas career suggests possible family trade in Bohemia/Moravia. Emigrated without family members.

grandparents.note: No Czech parish records accessed in this research pass. Requires primary record access at Zemský archiv v Opavě or Czech digitized archives.

village_of_origin.working_hypothesis_note: Circumstantial, well-supported by two independent lines of evidence; requires primary record to confirm

evidence.description: Czech surname database KdeJsme.cz (2016 population data) shows 88 total Czech Lepík bearers; Frýdek-Místek municipality holds 48 (54.5%), Frýdlant nad Ostravicí 11 (12.5%), Ostrava 13 (14.8%), all others 16 (18%). Frýdek-Místek area accounts for ~81% of all Czech Lepík bearers — extreme clustering indicating surname originated in that district.

Frank's wife Mary Mikeska came from a documented Moravian family. WikiTree Mikeska-77 (Josef Mikeska, 1830-1902) states origin 'Moravia, Austria.' WikiTree Mikeska-20 (Petr Mikeska, 1793–1864, miller) documents Zádveřice, Holešov, Moravia as family origin. WikiTree Mikeska-62 documents Alžběta (Mikeska) Hruska born 11 Feb 1852, Jablunce, Moravia. Both Lepík and Mikeska surnames cluster in the Moravian-Silesian Region on KdeJsme.cz. Chain migration pattern: same township → same Catholic church → suggests same or neighboring origin villages.

US census 'Bohemia' = generic label for all Czech-speaking lands (Bohemia, Moravia, Czech Silesia) per Wikipedia Czech Americans article and standard genealogical interpretation. Frank's census birthplace 'Bohemia, Austria' does not exclude Moravia or Czech Silesia.

candidate_villages.note: Largest concentration of Lepík surname in Czech Republic (54.5% of all bearers)
candidate_villages.historical_context: Historically split between Moravia (Místek) and Czech Silesia (Frýdek) at the Ostravice River; part of Austrian Empire Margraviate of Moravia and Duchy of Silesia as of 1862

candidate_villages.note: 8 km south of Frýdek-Místek; 12.5% of Czech Lepík bearers
Corroborating location: WikiTree Mikeska-62 documents Alžběta Mikeska born in 'Jablunce, Moravia' — may be Jablunkov (Frýdek-Místek District)

siblings_in_bohemia_moravia.note: Frank emigrated alone in 1881. No other Lepik/Lepick household found in Brown County KS 1895/1900 censuses. No newspaper reference to relatives arriving from Bohemia. Current Czech Republic has 88 Lepík bearers in Frýdek-Místek area who may descend from relatives who stayed.
siblings_in_bohemia_moravia.research_suggestion: Contact Czech genealogy forum Genealogie.cz or Czech Genealogical Society International to find living Lepík families in Frýdek-Místek area

czech_patrilineal_trace.potential_depth: Czech Catholic parish records in Frýdek-Místek area run from ~1630. At four generations per century, records may trace Lepík line back to approximately 1630–1700. Practical limit typically 1680s–1700s before records become incomplete.

generational_projection.record_type: Czech parish records early 18th century (may be incomplete)

surname_etymology.occupational_meaning: maker of sticky birdcatching traps or craftsman working with adhesive substances
surname_etymology.connection_to_frank: Consistent with Frank's lifelong occupation as shoemaker (cobbling requires glue/wax application)
surname_etymology.geographic_origin_type: Localized family surname — extreme geographic concentration suggests single-family origin in Frýdek-Místek district within last 400–500 years, not a widely distributed occupational surname

related_wikitree_profiles.name: Petr Mikeska
related_wikitree_profiles.relevance: Possible paternal grandfather of Josef Mikeska; Explore More includes Mary (Mikeska) Lepick (1863–1951)

related_wikitree_profiles.name: Alžběta (Mikeska) Hruska
related_wikitree_profiles.relevance: Different Mikeska branch; Jablunce = possibly Jablůnka (Vsetín District) or Jablunkov (Frýdek-Místek District) in Moravian-Silesian corridor

related_wikitree_profiles.name: Anna (Mikeska) Kovar
related_wikitree_profiles.relevance: Same Zádveřice-origin Mikeska cluster; census 'Böhmen' label applied to Moravian village confirms generic label issue

priority_order.what_to_request: Parish registers (matriky) for Místek, Frýdek, Frýdlant nad Ostravicí, Jablunkov — Catholic baptismal records 1855–1870 for Lepík surname

Online index search for Lepík surname in indexed parish records

census_records.note: Family had moved to Hazen, Arkansas ~1900–1901
